reasoning: The law is real, prabhu — this is what I voted for four years ago, not promises or temples or seats for Matua women in Rajya Sabha. My father can now apply for citizenship, there is an actual path in the government gazette that the Supreme Court has upheld. TMC had fifteen years and gave my father nothing but the Krishak Bandhu money — which is good, I do not deny it — but his name still sits nowhere on any citizenship paper. The BJP showed they can move mountains for what they believe in. Yes, only fourteen certificates in four years makes me wonder if they are also playing games, showing us enough to keep hope alive. But the law itself is solid, standing in court, and my father's chance is real for the first time in his life.
primary_drivers: The CAA law itself exists and is court-validated—my core demand
My father now has a legal pathway to citizenship, regardless of how slow implementation is
TMC's fifteen-year failure to address the citizenship issue despite holding power
